finding: A shell comment line beginning with # inside a fenced code block is parsed as a markdown heading by the task import parser. It resets the task declaration context, so a well-formed card with valid front matter fails with ATM_TASKS_PLAN_EMPTY even though the parser separately reports having detected the correct heading. The emitted diagnostic is also misleading: it reports ATM_TASK_IMPORT_REFERENCE_ONLY_ID_FRAGMENT naming the card id at a source line where that id does not appear.
expectedBehavior: Fence state must be tracked before heading detection: content inside a fenced block is never a heading and must not affect task declaration context. Diagnostics must cite a line that actually contains the referenced token.
evidenceOrRepro: Minimal repro: a card with valid front matter, a # ATM-GOV-XXXX heading, a first fenced bash block, a prose line, then a second fenced bash block. With second-block line "# register a holder intent on a literal path, then:" import dry-run returns ok:false ATM_TASKS_PLAN_PARSE_FAILED. Replacing that single line with "npm run build" returns ok:true. Same file, same front matter, only the leading # inside the fence differs. Encountered authoring ATM-GOV-0215; worked around by removing the comment, not by fixing the parser.
followUp: Consumer: TASK-ERR-0002 (error and recovery contract) owns the fence-before-heading ordering fix, the fixture with # comments in fenced blocks, and the diagnostic line-attribution correction. Do not open a standalone card and do not fold into ATM-GOV-0215; parser boundary is a separate family from broker overlap matcher convergence. Until fixed, any card carrying commented shell examples will silently fail to import.
